prompt.content:
Calculate and report the following metrics using the latest survey data:
1. Provider Insights
-Percentage of respondents using each data center provider
-A statement identifying the leading data center provider (by respondent share)
-A statement identifying the most frequently selected "most influential factor" among respondents who use the leading data center provider

2. Customer Behavior: 
- Overall average switching likelihood
- Average switching likelihood by sector (Public, Private, Other)
- Percentage of respondents with high switching propensity (i.e, selecting 4 or 5 for switching likelihood) 

Return outputs in a console message, with percentage values rounded to the nearest 0.01%, and all other numeric outputs rounded to two decimals.

rubrics.name: States the percentage of respondents using Digital Realty Trust is 32.00%... (ver_4a45dda7d62a4abb93e15de81f27040e)

messages.content: States the percentage of respondents using Digital Realty Trust is 32.00% (acceptable range 31.68% to 32.32%).

messages.content: States the percentage of respondents using In-House is a value other than 6.00%.

messages.content: States the leading data center provider (by respondent share) is Digital Realty Trust.

messages.content: States the most frequently selected "most influential factor" among respondents who use the leading data center provider is scalability.

golden_responses.golden_text_response:
Provider Insights
Percentage of respondents using Digital Realty Trust = 32.00%
Percentage of respondents using Equinix = 24.00%
Percentage of respondents using Iron Mountain = 16.00%
Percentage of respondents using CyrusOne = 22.00%
Percentage of respondents using In-House solutions = 6.00%
Leading data center provider (by respondent share) = Digital Realty Trust
Most influential factor selected by users of the leading provider = Scalability

Customer Behavior
Overall average switching likelihood (1–5 scale) = 2.96
Percentage of respondents with high switching propensity = 38.00%
Average switching likelihood among Public Sector respondents = 3.14
Average switching likelihood among Private Sector respondents = 3.03
Average switching likelihood among Other respondents = 2.00
